actionable_feedback: Update the Sensitivity worksheet to use relative +/-10% perturbations of the controlling thresholds (velocity and acceleration) and show resulting classification changes for each zone. Example: velocity threshold 6 -> test 5.4 and 6.6; accel 1.2 -> test 1.08 and 1.32. Document classification outcomes per perturbation.

Parameterize thresholds: create clearly labeled parameter cells (e.g., PARAM_VELOCITY_THRESH, PARAM_ACCEL_THRESH, PARAM_PRISM_VEL_THRESH) and reference those parameter cells in all Logic_Engine formulas. Remove hard-coded numeric thresholds from formulas.

In the workbook, add a Sensitivity_Summary table that explicitly lists the perturbed threshold values, the computed label per zone for each perturbation, and a short textual interpretation of any label changes.

Produce CSV exports (Packet_Input.csv and Logic_Engine.csv) alongside the .xlsx in the artifacts folder so automated verifiers (and environments lacking openpyxl) can inspect inputs and logic outputs. Include Decision_Summary.csv derived from the spreadsheet outputs.

Ensure the workbook is standard OOXML (no macros or nonstandard features) and include a small README.txt in artifacts describing parameter cell addresses and how to reproduce sensitivity checks with common tools (Excel, openpyxl, pandas).

Update the geotech_action_memo.md to note the parameter cell names/locations, describe the +/-10% sensitivity results (including any classification changes), and reference the CSV exports and README for automated verification.

Re-run the deterministic precheck and attach the CSV exports to the attempt so verifiers can parse and confirm parameterization and sensitivity. If possible, include a minimal Python validator script that reads the CSVs and reproduces the logic to demonstrate parser compatibility.
artifact_specific_comments: geotech_action_memo.md: clear and well-structured; explicitly documents trigger logic and per-zone decisions (Z1 PASS, Z2 FAIL, Z3 WARN). The Sensitivity checks section currently describes absolute +/-0.5 perturbations rather than relative +/-10%.

pit_slope_radar.xlsx: trace indicates workbook created with required worksheets and embedded formulas, but this environment could not parse the .xlsx (openpyxl_unavailable). Provide CSV exports and ensure standard OOXML compatibility for automated verification.
feedback_summary: Required artifacts present and logic correctly implemented. Score reduced to 0.75 because the delivered sensitivity analysis uses absolute +/-0.5 increments rather than the rubric-required +/-10% perturbations, and the workbook could not be programmatically parsed in this environment (openpyxl_unavailable) which limits automated verification of parameterized thresholds and cell-level formulas. Addressing the sensitivity format and enabling parser-friendly outputs should restore full credit.
